blockingIssues.description: The TUI footer still has a fixed six-row outer height, which leaves only four inner text rows inside the bordered keys block. Long routine feedback such as the runtime-active-account failure copy wraps at the validated terminal width, so one of the footer status/help lines becomes clipped again and the operator console still fails the footer-visibility contract.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When validating fixed-height TUI regions, explicitly exercise long wrapped operator feedback (not just short placeholder strings) so layout-fit checks prove realistic messages remain visible.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The follow-up review for `fix-tui-footer-and-passive-polling` found the new snapshot test only covered a short `Feedback line`, while real footer messages in `rust/proxypilot-rs/src/tui.rs` are long enough to wrap and clip help text again within the fixed six-row footer.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document when label-only TUI/runtime wording changes may use lightweight non-interactive verification instead of full `tuistory` validation, so narrow observability fixes can follow a clear exception path.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The pass review for `fix-runtime-account-count-semantics` noted the worker intentionally skipped manual `tuistory` verification because the change only aligned runtime/TUI labels, but the current skill does not describe that small-change exception.
